e_visa.nationality: All other nationalities not in Group 1, Group 2, or GCC
e_visa.notes: Nationalities not qualifying for visa-free entry may apply for an e-visa through the official Royal Oman Police e-visa portal evisa.rop.gov.om. The portal lists visa categories including Tourist Visit (Unsponsored and Sponsored), Resident Sponsored, and Visit Sponsored. Specific eligible nationalities are determined via the interactive eligibility wizard on the portal.

visa_policy.notes: Group 1 countries receive unconditional visa-free entry for 14 days (non-extendable, non-convertible to residence visa), unless superseded by a bilateral agreement granting longer stay (Turkey: 30 days; Russia: 30 days; Belarus: 30 days). Group 2 countries receive conditional visa-free entry for 14 days only if the holder also possesses a valid residency permit or valid visa from USA, UK, Canada, a Schengen country, Australia, or Japan, OR is a resident of a GCC member state. GCC citizens (Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE) enjoy unrestricted freedom of movement and right of residence with no visa required.

conditional_access.conditions: Group 2 – conditional visa-free entry for 14 days, non-extendable. Requires valid residency permit or valid visa from USA, UK, Canada, Schengen area, Australia, or Japan. Traveller must be a national of a Group 2 country AND hold a valid US visa. Official text: 'citizens of the second list countries are required to be either residents or have a valid entry visa for one of the following countries (the United States of America, Canada, Australia, the United Kingdom, Schengen Agreement countries, Japan), or to be residents of one of the countries The Gulf Cooperation Council'.
Group 2 – conditional visa-free entry for 14 days, non-extendable. Traveller must be a national of a Group 2 country AND hold a valid Canadian visa.
Group 2 – conditional visa-free entry for 14 days, non-extendable. Traveller must be a national of a Group 2 country AND hold a valid UK visa.
Group 2 – conditional visa-free entry for 14 days, non-extendable. Traveller must be a national of a Group 2 country AND hold a valid Schengen area visa.
Group 2 – conditional visa-free entry for 14 days, non-extendable. Traveller must be a national of a Group 2 country AND hold a valid Australian visa.
Group 2 – conditional visa-free entry for 14 days, non-extendable. Traveller must be a national of a Group 2 country AND hold a valid Japanese visa.
Group 2 – conditional visa-free entry for 14 days, non-extendable. Traveller must be a national of a Group 2 country AND hold a valid US residency permit.
Group 2 – conditional visa-free entry for 14 days, non-extendable. Traveller must be a national of a Group 2 country AND hold a valid Canadian residency permit.

Group 2 – conditional visa-free entry for 14 days, non-extendable. Traveller must be a national of a Group 2 country AND hold a valid Schengen area residency permit.

Group 2 – conditional visa-free entry for 14 days, non-extendable. Traveller must be a national of a Group 2 country AND hold a valid Japanese residency permit.

credential.subtype: any valid GCC residency permit (approved profession, min. 3 months validity)

conditional_access.conditions: Group 2 – conditional visa-free entry for 14 days, non-extendable. Traveller must be a national of a Group 2 country AND be a resident of one of the GCC countries with qualifying professional status. Official text: 'or to be residents of one of the countries The Gulf Cooperation Council and its profession are among the professions that benefit from the resident visa'.

credential.subtype: GCC resident permit, approved profession, valid ≥3 months (Visa Type 29A)

conditional_access.conditions: Any nationality (not otherwise visa-free) holding a valid GCC country residence permit with an approved profession and minimum 3 months validity may obtain a Tourist Visa on Arrival (Type 29A) at Oman airports and land borders, or apply for the equivalent e-visa at evisa.rop.gov.om. Fee: OMR 5. Companions (Type 29B) also eligible.

Mutual short-stay visa exemption for holders of Diplomatic, Special, and Service Passports under agreement signed in New York on 23 September 2024. Ratified by Royal Decree 57/2024 (issued 6 November 2024). Maximum stay duration not stated verbatim in publicly accessible decree summary.
Mutual visa exemption for holders of Diplomatic, Special, and Service Passports under agreement signed in New York on 23 September 2024. Ratified by Royal Decree 55/2024.
Mutual visa exemption for holders of Diplomatic, Special, and Service Passports under agreement signed in New York on 26 September 2024. Ratified by Royal Decree 56/2024.

Mutual visa exemption for holders of Diplomatic, Official, Special, and Service Passports under agreement signed in Muscat on 9 December 2024. Ratified by Royal Decree 20/2025, published in Official Gazette No. 1583 on 9 February 2025.
Mutual visa exemption for holders of Diplomatic, Special, and Service Passports under agreement signed in Salalah on 3 September 2025. Ratified by Royal Decree 100/2025.
Mutual visa exemption for holders of Diplomatic, Special, and Service Passports under agreement ratified by Royal Decree 91/2025 (issued 21 October 2025).
Mutual visa-free travel for diplomatic and service passport holders. Agreement signed in Madrid on 5 November 2025, entered into force upon publication in Oman's Official Gazette on 4 January 2026.

cbi.notes: Oman does not offer a citizenship by investment program. The Golden Residency Programme grants renewable long-term residency only, with no direct pathway to citizenship through investment. Natural citizenship requires 15+ years of continuous legal residence with no single absence exceeding 90 days per year.

programs.name: Oman Golden Residency Programme (10-Year Residency)
programs.type: real_estate / business / bonds / fund / deposit / employment
programs.notes: Renewable 10-year residency for international investors, entrepreneurs, and high-net-worth individuals. Seven qualifying investment routes: (1) Real estate in Integrated Tourism Complexes; (2) Company formation in Oman; (3) Government Development Bonds; (4) Muscat Stock Exchange shares; (5) Fixed-term bank deposit (minimum 5 years); (6) Owning a company employing 50+ Omani nationals; (7) Nomination under Foreign Capital Investment Law. Minimum capital threshold: OMR 200,000 (approx. USD 520,000) per investoman.om. Benefits include full foreign ownership, up to 3 domestic workers, sponsorship of spouse and children, fast-track immigration lanes. No direct path to citizenship through this program.

agreements: GCC (Gulf Cooperation Council) – freedom of movement and right of residence for GCC nationals among member states (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE)

validation_notes: CORRECTED: Turkey max_stay_days updated from 14 to 30. Bilateral mutual visa exemption (ordinary passports) entered into force November 1, 2025; announced December 22, 2025 on fm.gov.om/en/33835/. Stay is 30 consecutive days per visit, max 90 days within any 180-day period. The entry-visas page still lists Turkey in Group 1 at 14 days, but the bilateral agreement supersedes this.

CORRECTED: Russia max_stay_days updated from 14 to 30. Bilateral mutual visa exemption (ordinary passports) effective July 18, 2025. Agreement signed Moscow April 22, 2025; ratified by Royal Decree 53/2025. Stay is 30 days per visit, max 90 days per calendar year. The entry-visas page still shows Russia in Group 1 at 14 days but the bilateral agreement supersedes this.

CORRECTED: Belarus max_stay_days updated from 14 to 30. Bilateral mutual visa exemption (ordinary passports) effective March 17, 2026 per fm.gov.om/en/39389/. Stay is 30 consecutive days per visit, max 90 days per calendar year. Not for business/paid activities.

CONFIRMED: Philippines remains in Group 1 at 14 days. A December 2025 announcement (fm.gov.om/en/32199/) confirmed Philippines citizens exempt from short-visit visa requirements from 2026, consistent with existing Group 1 listing.
CONFIRMED: Group 2 country list and conditions match the live fm.gov.om/en/visitors/entry-visas/ page exactly.
CONFIRMED: GCC citizen freedom-of-movement entry is correct.
CONFIRMED: GCC resident visa on arrival (Type 29A) confirmed via gov.om/en/w/get-a-gcc-resident-visa; fee OMR 5, 28-day stay, approved profession required.

NOTE: The fm.gov.om entry-visas page does not yet reflect updated bilateral stay durations for Russia, Turkey, and Belarus — the page still lists all Group 1 countries at 14 days. The bilateral-specific stay durations are sourced from dedicated fm.gov.om bilateral announcement pages.

CONFIRMED (2026-06-14): India (IND) classification as 'conditional' is correct. India is an Oman Group 2 country per fm.gov.om/en/visitors/entry-visas/ — ordinary passport holders may enter visa-free for 14 days ONLY if holding a valid visa or residence permit from USA, UK, Canada, Schengen area, Australia, or Japan, or if resident in a GCC state. Without such credentials, Indians must apply for an e-visa via evisa.rop.gov.om. Aggregator claim of visa-free or visa-on-arrival for India is incorrect. Source: https://www.fm.gov.om/en/visitors/entry-visas/
gaps: The evisa.rop.gov.om eligibility wizard is interactive and does not expose a static list of e-visa eligible nationalities; the full list of nationalities eligible for e-visa vs. requiring embassy visa could not be scraped from official pages.

Specific investment minimum amounts per category for the Golden Residency Programme (e.g., separate real estate vs. bonds thresholds) were not listed verbatim on omanresidence.gov.om; only the aggregate OMR 200,000 threshold appeared on investoman.om.
Dual citizenship policy for Oman not confirmed from an official government source.
Diplomatic passport waiver for Mongolia: maximum stay duration not accessible publicly on decree.om (subscription required); lawrtal.com used as secondary source, source_official=false.
Diplomatic passport waiver for Spain: no direct official Omani government URL found for this specific decree at time of research; visahq.com used as secondary source, source_official=false.
GCC resident visa approved professions list (139 professions per ROP platform) not fully enumerated from official sources; only the general framework confirmed from gov.om.

The fm.gov.om entry-visas page has not been updated to reflect bilateral stay durations for Turkey, Russia, and Belarus — it still shows all Group 1 countries at 14 days. The actual permissible stay for these three countries (30 days) is sourced from separate bilateral announcement pages on fm.gov.om.
Philippines visa exemption announcement (fm.gov.om/en/32199/) confirms visa-free status from 2026 but does not specify the exact maximum stay duration.
Fast-track, digital nomad, and skilled talent visa programs referenced in media but no official government page with binding details found; omitted.
'French Guinea' listing on official source is ambiguous — may refer to French Guiana (an overseas territory of France, not a sovereign state); no iso3 assigned.

outbound_official.note: No official Oman government outbound visa-list page located. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs page at fm.gov.om/en/citizens/visa-requirements/ explicitly states visa regulations are complex and directs Omani citizens to check with individual destination-country embassies/consulates or use the IATA Travel Centre — it does not enumerate countries by visa category. No equivalent of India's mea.gov.in/vffin page exists on fm.gov.om, mofa.gov.om, rop.gov.om, or any other official Omani government domain. Rely on destination-side data (inversion of inbound visa_policy records).
